speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Noble patricians, patrons of my right,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Defend the justice of my cause with arms.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: And countrymen, my loving followers,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Plead my successive title with your swords.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: I am his firstborn son that was the last

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: That wore the imperial diadem of Rome.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Then let my father’s honors live in me,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Nor wrong mine age with this indignity.

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Romans, friends, followers, favorers of my right,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: If ever Bassianus, Caesar’s son,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Were gracious in the eyes of royal Rome,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Keep, then, this passage to the Capitol,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: And suffer not dishonor to approach

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: The imperial seat, to virtue consecrate,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: To justice, continence, and nobility;

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: But let desert in pure election shine,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: And, Romans, fight for freedom in your choice.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Princes that strive by factions and by friends

speaker: MARCUS
text: Ambitiously for rule and empery,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Know that the people of Rome, for whom we stand

speaker: MARCUS
text: A special party, have by common voice,

speaker: MARCUS
text: In election for the Roman empery,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Chosen Andronicus, surnamèd Pius

speaker: MARCUS
text: For many good and great deserts to Rome.

speaker: MARCUS
text: A nobler man, a braver warrior,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Lives not this day within the city walls.

speaker: MARCUS
text: He by the Senate is accited home

speaker: MARCUS
text: From weary wars against the barbarous Goths,

speaker: MARCUS
text: That with his sons, a terror to our foes,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Hath yoked a nation strong, trained up in arms.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Ten years are spent since first he undertook

speaker: MARCUS
text: This cause of Rome, and chastisèd with arms

speaker: MARCUS
text: Our enemies’ pride. Five times he hath returned

speaker: MARCUS
text: Bleeding to Rome, bearing his valiant sons

speaker: MARCUS
text: In coffins from the field.

speaker: MARCUS
text: And now at last, laden with honor’s spoils,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Returns the good Andronicus to Rome,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Renownèd Titus flourishing in arms.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Let us entreat, by honor of his name

speaker: MARCUS
text: Whom worthily you would have now succeed,

speaker: MARCUS
text: And in the Capitol and Senate’s right,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Whom you pretend to honor and adore,

speaker: MARCUS
text: That you withdraw you and abate your strength,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Dismiss your followers and, as suitors should,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Plead your deserts in peace and humbleness.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: How fair the tribune speaks to calm my thoughts!

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Marcus Andronicus, so I do affy

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: In thy uprightness and integrity,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: And so I love and honor thee and thine,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Thy noble brother Titus and his sons,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: And her to whom my thoughts are humbled all,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Gracious Lavinia, Rome’s rich ornament,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: That I will here dismiss my loving friends,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: And to my fortunes and the people’s favor

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Commit my cause in balance to be weighed.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Friends that have been thus forward in my right,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: I thank you all and here dismiss you all,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: And to the love and favor of my country

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Commit myself, my person, and the cause.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Rome, be as just and gracious unto me

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: As I am confident and kind to thee.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Open the gates and let me in.

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Tribunes, and me, a poor competitor.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Romans, make way! The good Andronicus,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Patron of virtue, Rome’s best champion,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Successful in the battles that he fights,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: With honor and with fortune is returned

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: From where he circumscribèd with his sword

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: And brought to yoke the enemies of Rome.

speaker: TITUS
text: Hail Rome, victorious in thy mourning weeds!

speaker: TITUS
text: Lo, as the bark that hath discharged his fraught

speaker: TITUS
text: Returns with precious lading to the bay

speaker: TITUS
text: From whence at first she weighed her anchorage,

speaker: TITUS
text: Cometh Andronicus, bound with laurel boughs,

speaker: TITUS
text: To resalute his country with his tears,

speaker: TITUS
text: Tears of true joy for his return to Rome.

speaker: TITUS
text: Thou great defender of this Capitol,

speaker: TITUS
text: Stand gracious to the rites that we intend.

speaker: TITUS
text: Romans, of five-and-twenty valiant sons,

speaker: TITUS
text: Half of the number that King Priam had,

speaker: TITUS
text: Behold the poor remains alive and dead.

speaker: TITUS
text: These that survive let Rome reward with love;

speaker: TITUS
text: These that I bring unto their latest home,

speaker: TITUS
text: With burial amongst their ancestors.

speaker: TITUS
text: Here Goths have given me leave to sheathe my sword.

speaker: TITUS
text: Titus, unkind and careless of thine own,

speaker: TITUS
text: Why suffer’st thou thy sons unburied yet

speaker: TITUS
text: To hover on the dreadful shore of Styx?

speaker: TITUS
text: Make way to lay them by their brethren.

speaker: TITUS
text: There greet in silence, as the dead are wont,

speaker: TITUS
text: And sleep in peace, slain in your country’s wars.

speaker: TITUS
text: O sacred receptacle of my joys,

speaker: TITUS
text: Sweet cell of virtue and nobility,

speaker: TITUS
text: How many sons hast thou of mine in store

speaker: TITUS
text: That thou wilt never render to me more?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Give us the proudest prisoner of the Goths,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That we may hew his limbs and on a pile,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Ad manes fratrum, sacrifice his flesh

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Before this earthy prison of their bones,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That so the shadows be not unappeased,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Nor we disturbed with prodigies on Earth.

speaker: TITUS
text: I give him you, the noblest that survives,

speaker: TITUS
text: The eldest son of this distressèd queen.

speaker: TAMORA
text: Stay, Roman brethren!—Gracious conqueror,

speaker: TAMORA
text: Victorious Titus, rue the tears I shed,

speaker: TAMORA
text: A mother’s tears in passion for her son.

speaker: TAMORA
text: And if thy sons were ever dear to thee,

speaker: TAMORA
text: O think my son to be as dear to me.

speaker: TAMORA
text: Sufficeth not that we are brought to Rome

speaker: TAMORA
text: To beautify thy triumphs and return

speaker: TAMORA
text: Captive to thee and to thy Roman yoke,

speaker: TAMORA
text: But must my sons be slaughtered in the streets

speaker: TAMORA
text: For valiant doings in their country’s cause?

speaker: TAMORA
text: O, if to fight for king and commonweal

speaker: TAMORA
text: Were piety in thine, it is in these!

speaker: TAMORA
text: Andronicus, stain not thy tomb with blood.

speaker: TAMORA
text: Wilt thou draw near the nature of the gods?

speaker: TAMORA
text: Draw near them then in being merciful.

speaker: TAMORA
text: Sweet mercy is nobility’s true badge.

speaker: TAMORA
text: Thrice-noble Titus, spare my first-born son.

speaker: TITUS
text: Patient yourself, madam, and pardon me.

speaker: TITUS
text: These are their brethren whom your Goths beheld

speaker: TITUS
text: Alive and dead, and for their brethren slain

speaker: TITUS
text: Religiously they ask a sacrifice.

speaker: TITUS
text: To this your son is marked, and die he must,

speaker: TITUS
text: T’ appease their groaning shadows that are gone.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Away with him, and make a fire straight,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And with our swords upon a pile of wood

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Let’s hew his limbs till they be clean consumed.

speaker: TAMORA
text: O cruel, irreligious piety!

speaker: CHIRON
text: Was never Scythia half so barbarous!

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Oppose not Scythia to ambitious Rome!

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Alarbus goes to rest and we survive

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: To tremble under Titus’ threat’ning look.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Then, madam, stand resolved, but hope withal

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: The selfsame gods that armed the Queen of Troy

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: With opportunity of sharp revenge

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Upon the Thracian tyrant in his tent

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: May favor Tamora the Queen of Goths

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: (When Goths were Goths, and Tamora was queen)

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: To quit the bloody wrongs upon her foes.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: See, lord and father, how we have performed

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Our Roman rites. Alarbus’ limbs are lopped,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And entrails feed the sacrificing fire,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Whose smoke like incense doth perfume the sky.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Remaineth naught but to inter our brethren,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And with loud larums welcome them to Rome.

speaker: TITUS
text: Let it be so. And let Andronicus

speaker: TITUS
text: Make this his latest farewell to their souls.

speaker: TITUS
text: In peace and honor rest you here, my sons,

speaker: TITUS
text: Rome’s readiest champions, repose you here in rest,

speaker: TITUS
text: Secure from worldly chances and mishaps.

speaker: TITUS
text: Here lurks no treason, here no envy swells,

speaker: TITUS
text: Here grow no damnèd drugs; here are no storms,

speaker: TITUS
text: No noise, but silence and eternal sleep.

speaker: TITUS
text: In peace and honor rest you here, my sons.

speaker: LAVINIA
text: In peace and honor live Lord Titus long;

speaker: LAVINIA
text: My noble lord and father, live in fame.

speaker: LAVINIA
text: Lo, at this tomb my tributary tears

speaker: LAVINIA
text: I render for my brethren’s obsequies,

speaker: LAVINIA
text: And at thy feet I kneel, with tears of joy

speaker: LAVINIA
text: Shed on this earth for thy return to Rome.

speaker: LAVINIA
text: O bless me here with thy victorious hand,

speaker: LAVINIA
text: Whose fortunes Rome’s best citizens applaud.

speaker: TITUS
text: Kind Rome, that hast thus lovingly reserved

speaker: TITUS
text: The cordial of mine age to glad my heart!—

speaker: TITUS
text: Lavinia, live, outlive thy father’s days

speaker: TITUS
text: And fame’s eternal date, for virtue’s praise.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Long live Lord Titus, my belovèd brother,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Gracious triumpher in the eyes of Rome.

speaker: TITUS
text: Thanks, gentle tribune, noble brother Marcus.

speaker: MARCUS
text: And welcome, nephews, from successful wars—

speaker: MARCUS
text: You that survive, and you that sleep in fame.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Fair lords, your fortunes are alike in all,

speaker: MARCUS
text: That in your country’s service drew your swords;

speaker: MARCUS
text: But safer triumph is this funeral pomp,

speaker: MARCUS
text: That hath aspired to Solon’s happiness,

speaker: MARCUS
text: And triumphs over chance in honor’s bed.—

speaker: MARCUS
text: Titus Andronicus, the people of Rome,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Whose friend in justice thou hast ever been,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Send thee by me, their tribune and their trust,

speaker: MARCUS
text: This palliament of white and spotless hue,

speaker: MARCUS
text: And name thee in election for the empire

speaker: MARCUS
text: With these our late deceasèd emperor’s sons.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Be candidatus, then, and put it on

speaker: MARCUS
text: And help to set a head on headless Rome.

speaker: TITUS
text: A better head her glorious body fits

speaker: TITUS
text: Than his that shakes for age and feebleness.

speaker: TITUS
text:
To
                 
                Tribunes
                 
                and
                 
                Senators
                 
                aloft
                .
                 
              What, should I don this robe and trouble you?

speaker: TITUS
text: Be chosen with proclamations today,

speaker: TITUS
text: Tomorrow yield up rule, resign my life,

speaker: TITUS
text: And set abroad new business for you all?

speaker: TITUS
text: Rome, I have been thy soldier forty years,

speaker: TITUS
text: And led my country’s strength successfully,

speaker: TITUS
text: And buried one and twenty valiant sons,

speaker: TITUS
text: Knighted in field, slain manfully in arms,

speaker: TITUS
text: In right and service of their noble country.

speaker: TITUS
text: Give me a staff of honor for mine age,

speaker: TITUS
text: But not a scepter to control the world.

speaker: TITUS
text: Upright he held it, lords, that held it last.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Titus, thou shalt obtain and ask the empery.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Proud and ambitious tribune, canst thou tell?

speaker: TITUS
text: Patience, Prince Saturninus.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Romans, do me right.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Patricians, draw your swords and sheathe them not

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Till Saturninus be Rome’s emperor.—

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Andronicus, would thou were shipped to hell

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Rather than rob me of the people’s hearts.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Proud Saturnine, interrupter of the good

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That noble-minded Titus means to thee.

speaker: TITUS
text: Content thee, prince. I will restore to thee

speaker: TITUS
text: The people’s hearts and wean them from themselves.

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Andronicus, I do not flatter thee,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: But honor thee, and will do till I die.

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: My faction if thou strengthen with thy friends,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: I will most thankful be, and thanks, to men

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Of noble minds, is honorable meed.

speaker: TITUS
text: People of Rome, and people’s tribunes here,

speaker: TITUS
text: I ask your voices and your suffrages.

speaker: TITUS
text: Will you bestow them friendly on Andronicus?

speaker: TRIBUNES
text: To gratify the good Andronicus

speaker: TRIBUNES
text: And gratulate his safe return to Rome,

speaker: TRIBUNES
text: The people will accept whom he admits.

speaker: TITUS
text: Tribunes, I thank you, and this suit I make:

speaker: TITUS
text: That you create our emperor’s eldest son,

speaker: TITUS
text: Lord Saturnine, whose virtues will, I hope,

speaker: TITUS
text: Reflect on Rome as Titan’s rays on Earth

speaker: TITUS
text: And ripen justice in this commonweal.

speaker: TITUS
text: Then, if you will elect by my advice,

speaker: TITUS
text: Crown him and say “Long live our emperor.”

speaker: MARCUS
text: With voices and applause of every sort,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Patricians and plebeians, we create

speaker: MARCUS
text: Lord Saturninus Rome’s great emperor,

speaker: MARCUS
text: And say “Long live our Emperor Saturnine.”

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Titus Andronicus, for thy favors done

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: To us in our election this day,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: I give thee thanks in part of thy deserts,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: And will with deeds requite thy gentleness.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: And for an onset, Titus, to advance

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Thy name and honorable family,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Lavinia will I make my empress,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Rome’s royal mistress, mistress of my heart,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: And in the sacred Pantheon her espouse.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Tell me, Andronicus, doth this motion please thee?

speaker: TITUS
text: It doth, my worthy lord, and in this match

speaker: TITUS
text: I hold me highly honored of your Grace;

speaker: TITUS
text: And here in sight of Rome to Saturnine,

speaker: TITUS
text: King and commander of our commonweal,

speaker: TITUS
text: The wide world’s emperor, do I consecrate

speaker: TITUS
text: My sword, my chariot, and my prisoners,

speaker: TITUS
text: Presents well worthy Rome’s imperious lord.

speaker: TITUS
text: Receive them, then, the tribute that I owe,

speaker: TITUS
text: Mine honor’s ensigns humbled at thy feet.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Thanks, noble Titus, father of my life.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: How proud I am of thee and of thy gifts

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Rome shall record.—And when I do forget

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: The least of these unspeakable deserts,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Romans, forget your fealty to me.

speaker: TITUS
text: Now, madam, are you prisoner to an emperor,

speaker: TITUS
text: To him that for your honor and your state

speaker: TITUS
text: Will use you nobly, and your followers.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: A goodly lady, trust me, of the hue

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: That I would choose, were I to choose anew.—

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Clear up, fair queen, that cloudy countenance.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Though chance of war hath wrought this change of cheer,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Thou com’st not to be made a scorn in Rome.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Princely shall be thy usage every way.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Rest on my word, and let not discontent

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Daunt all your hopes. Madam, he comforts you

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Can make you greater than the Queen of Goths.—

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Lavinia, you are not displeased with this?

speaker: LAVINIA
text: Not I, my lord, sith true nobility

speaker: LAVINIA
text: Warrants these words in princely courtesy.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Thanks, sweet Lavinia.—Romans, let us go.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Ransomless here we set our prisoners free.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Proclaim our honors, lords, with trump and drum.

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Lord Titus, by your leave, this maid is mine.

speaker: TITUS
text: How, sir? Are you in earnest then, my lord?

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Ay, noble Titus, and resolved withal

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: To do myself this reason and this right.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Suum cuique is our Roman justice.

speaker: MARCUS
text: This prince in justice seizeth but his own.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And that he will and shall, if Lucius live!

speaker: TITUS
text: Traitors, avaunt! Where is the Emperor’s guard?

speaker: TITUS
text: Treason, my lord. Lavinia is surprised.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Surprised? By whom?

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: By him that justly may

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Bear his betrothed from all the world away.

speaker: MUTIUS
text: Brothers, help to convey her hence away,

speaker: MUTIUS
text: And with my sword I’ll keep this door safe.

speaker: TITUS
text: Follow, my lord, and I’ll soon bring her back.

speaker: MUTIUS
text: My lord, you pass not here.

speaker: TITUS
text: What, villain boy,

speaker: TITUS
text: Barr’st me my way in Rome?

speaker: MUTIUS
text: Help, Lucius, help!

speaker: LUCIUS
text: My lord, you are unjust, and more than so!

speaker: LUCIUS
text: In wrongful quarrel you have slain your son.

speaker: TITUS
text: Nor thou nor he are any sons of mine.

speaker: TITUS
text: My sons would never so dishonor me.

speaker: TITUS
text: Traitor, restore Lavinia to the Emperor.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Dead if you will, but not to be his wife

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That is another’s lawful promised love.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: No, Titus, no, the Emperor needs her not,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Nor her, nor thee, nor any of thy stock.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: I’ll trust by leisure him that mocks me once,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Thee never, nor thy traitorous haughty sons,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Confederates all thus to dishonor me.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Was none in Rome to make a stale

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: But Saturnine? Full well, Andronicus,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Agree these deeds with that proud brag of thine

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: That said’st I begged the empire at thy hands.

speaker: TITUS
text: O monstrous! What reproachful words are these?

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: But go thy ways. Go give that changing piece

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: To him that flourished for her with his sword.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: A valiant son-in-law thou shalt enjoy,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: One fit to bandy with thy lawless sons,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: To ruffle in the commonwealth of Rome.

speaker: TITUS
text: These words are razors to my wounded heart.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: And therefore, lovely Tamora, Queen of Goths,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: That like the stately Phoebe ’mongst her nymphs

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Dost overshine the gallant’st dames of Rome,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: If thou be pleased with this my sudden choice,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Behold, I choose thee, Tamora, for my bride,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: And will create thee Emperess of Rome.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Speak, Queen of Goths, dost thou applaud my choice?

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: And here I swear by all the Roman gods,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Sith priest and holy water are so near,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: And tapers burn so bright, and everything

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: In readiness for Hymenaeus stand,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: I will not resalute the streets of Rome

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Or climb my palace till from forth this place

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: I lead espoused my bride along with me.

speaker: TAMORA
text: And here in sight of heaven to Rome I swear,

speaker: TAMORA
text: If Saturnine advance the Queen of Goths,

speaker: TAMORA
text: She will a handmaid be to his desires,

speaker: TAMORA
text: A loving nurse, a mother to his youth.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Ascend, fair queen, to Pantheon.—Lords, accompany

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Your noble emperor and his lovely bride,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Sent by the heavens for Prince Saturnine,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Whose wisdom hath her fortune conquerèd.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: There shall we consummate our spousal rites.

speaker: TITUS
text: I am not bid to wait upon this bride.

speaker: TITUS
text: Titus, when wert thou wont to walk alone,

speaker: TITUS
text: Dishonored thus and challengèd of wrongs?

speaker: MARCUS
text: O Titus, see! O, see what thou hast done!

speaker: MARCUS
text: In a bad quarrel slain a virtuous son.

speaker: TITUS
text: No, foolish tribune, no; no son of mine,

speaker: TITUS
text: Nor thou, nor these confederates in the deed

speaker: TITUS
text: That hath dishonored all our family.

speaker: TITUS
text: Unworthy brother and unworthy sons!

speaker: LUCIUS
text: But let us give him burial as becomes,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Give Mutius burial with our brethren.

speaker: TITUS
text: Traitors, away! He rests not in this tomb.

speaker: TITUS
text: This monument five hundred years hath stood,

speaker: TITUS
text: Which I have sumptuously reedified.

speaker: TITUS
text: Here none but soldiers and Rome’s servitors

speaker: TITUS
text: Repose in fame, none basely slain in brawls.

speaker: TITUS
text: Bury him where you can. He comes not here.

speaker: MARCUS
text: My lord, this is impiety in you.

speaker: MARCUS
text: My nephew Mutius’ deeds do plead for him.

speaker: MARCUS
text: He must be buried with his brethren.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And shall, or him we will accompany.

speaker: TITUS
text: “And shall”? What villain was it spake that word?

speaker: MARTIUS
text: He that would vouch it in any place but here.

speaker: TITUS
text: What, would you bury him in my despite?

speaker: MARCUS
text: No, noble Titus, but entreat of thee

speaker: MARCUS
text: To pardon Mutius and to bury him.

speaker: TITUS
text: Marcus, even thou hast struck upon my crest,

speaker: TITUS
text: And with these boys mine honor thou hast wounded.

speaker: TITUS
text: My foes I do repute you every one.

speaker: TITUS
text: So trouble me no more, but get you gone.

speaker: QUINTUS
text: He is not with himself; let us withdraw.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Not I, till Mutius’ bones be burièd.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Brother, for in that name doth nature plead—

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Father, and in that name doth nature speak—

speaker: TITUS
text: Speak thou no more, if all the rest will speed.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Renownèd Titus, more than half my soul—

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Dear father, soul and substance of us all—

speaker: MARCUS
text: Suffer thy brother Marcus to inter

speaker: MARCUS
text: His noble nephew here in virtue’s nest,

speaker: MARCUS
text: That died in honor and Lavinia’s cause.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Thou art a Roman; be not barbarous.

speaker: MARCUS
text: The Greeks upon advice did bury Ajax,

speaker: MARCUS
text: That slew himself, and wise Laertes’ son

speaker: MARCUS
text: Did graciously plead for his funerals.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Let not young Mutius, then, that was thy joy,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Be barred his entrance here.

speaker: TITUS
text: Rise, Marcus, rise.

speaker: TITUS
text: The dismall’st day is this that e’er I saw,

speaker: TITUS
text: To be dishonored by my sons in Rome.

speaker: TITUS
text: Well, bury him, and bury me the next.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: There lie thy bones, sweet Mutius, with thy friends’,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Till we with trophies do adorn thy tomb.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: No man shed tears for noble Mutius.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: He lives in fame, that died in virtue’s cause.

speaker: MARCUS
text: My lord, to step out of these dreary dumps,

speaker: MARCUS
text: How comes it that the subtle Queen of Goths

speaker: MARCUS
text: Is of a sudden thus advanced in Rome?

speaker: TITUS
text: I know not, Marcus, but I know it is.

speaker: TITUS
text: Whether by device or no, the heavens can tell.

speaker: TITUS
text: Is she not then beholding to the man

speaker: TITUS
text: That brought her for this high good turn so far?

speaker: TITUS
text: Yes, and will nobly him remunerate.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: So, Bassianus, you have played your prize.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: God give you joy, sir, of your gallant bride.

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: And you of yours, my lord. I say no more,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Nor wish no less, and so I take my leave.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Traitor, if Rome have law or we have power,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Thou and thy faction shall repent this rape.

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: “Rape” call you it, my lord, to seize my own,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: My true betrothèd love and now my wife?

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: But let the laws of Rome determine all.

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Meanwhile am I possessed of that is mine.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: ’Tis good, sir, you are very short with us.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: But if we live, we’ll be as sharp with you.

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: My lord, what I have done, as best I may,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Answer I must, and shall do with my life.

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Only thus much I give your Grace to know:

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: By all the duties that I owe to Rome,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: This noble gentleman, Lord Titus here,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Is in opinion and in honor wronged,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: That in the rescue of Lavinia

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: With his own hand did slay his youngest son,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: In zeal to you, and highly moved to wrath

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: To be controlled in that he frankly gave.

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: Receive him then to favor, Saturnine,

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: That hath expressed himself in all his deeds

speaker: BASSIANUS
text: A father and a friend to thee and Rome.

speaker: TITUS
text: Prince Bassianus, leave to plead my deeds.

speaker: TITUS
text: ’Tis thou, and those, that have dishonored me.

speaker: TITUS
text: Rome and the righteous heavens be my judge

speaker: TITUS
text: How I have loved and honored Saturnine.

speaker: TAMORA
text: My worthy lord, if ever Tamora

speaker: TAMORA
text: Were gracious in those princely eyes of thine,

speaker: TAMORA
text: Then hear me speak indifferently for all,

speaker: TAMORA
text: And at my suit, sweet, pardon what is past.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: What, madam, be dishonored openly,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: And basely put it up without revenge?

speaker: TAMORA
text: Not so, my lord; the gods of Rome forfend

speaker: TAMORA
text: I should be author to dishonor you.

speaker: TAMORA
text: But on mine honor dare I undertake

speaker: TAMORA
text: For good Lord Titus’ innocence in all,

speaker: TAMORA
text: Whose fury not dissembled speaks his griefs.

speaker: TAMORA
text: Then at my suit look graciously on him.

speaker: TAMORA
text: Lose not so noble a friend on vain suppose,

speaker: TAMORA
text: Nor with sour looks afflict his gentle heart.

speaker: TAMORA
text:
Aside
                 
                to
                 
                Saturninus
                .
                 
              My lord, be ruled by me; be won at last.

speaker: TAMORA
text: Dissemble all your griefs and discontents.

speaker: TAMORA
text: You are but newly planted in your throne.

speaker: TAMORA
text: Lest, then, the people, and patricians too,

speaker: TAMORA
text: Upon a just survey take Titus’ part

speaker: TAMORA
text: And so supplant you for ingratitude,

speaker: TAMORA
text: Which Rome reputes to be a heinous sin.

speaker: TAMORA
text: Yield at entreats, and then let me alone.

speaker: TAMORA
text: I’ll find a day to massacre them all

speaker: TAMORA
text: And raze their faction and their family,

speaker: TAMORA
text: The cruel father and his traitorous sons,

speaker: TAMORA
text: To whom I sued for my dear son’s life,

speaker: TAMORA
text: And make them know what ’tis to let a queen

speaker: TAMORA
text: Kneel in the streets and beg for grace in vain.

speaker: TAMORA
text:
Aloud
                .
                 
              Come, come, sweet emperor.—Come,Andronicus.—

speaker: TAMORA
text: Take up this good old man, and cheer the heart

speaker: TAMORA
text: That dies in tempest of thy angry frown.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Rise, Titus, rise. My empress hath prevailed.

speaker: TITUS
text: I thank your Majesty and her, my lord.

speaker: TITUS
text: These words, these looks, infuse new life in me.

speaker: TAMORA
text: Titus, I am incorporate in Rome,

speaker: TAMORA
text: A Roman now adopted happily,

speaker: TAMORA
text: And must advise the Emperor for his good.

speaker: TAMORA
text: This day all quarrels die, Andronicus.—

speaker: TAMORA
text: And let it be mine honor, good my lord,

speaker: TAMORA
text: That I have reconciled your friends and you.—

speaker: TAMORA
text: For you, Prince Bassianus, I have passed

speaker: TAMORA
text: My word and promise to the Emperor

speaker: TAMORA
text: That you will be more mild and tractable.—

speaker: TAMORA
text: And fear not, lords—and you, Lavinia.

speaker: TAMORA
text: By my advice, all humbled on your knees,

speaker: TAMORA
text: You shall ask pardon of his Majesty.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: We do, and vow to heaven and to his Highness

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That what we did was mildly as we might,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Tend’ring our sister’s honor and our own.

speaker: MARCUS
text: That on mine honor here do I protest.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Away, and talk not; trouble us no more.

speaker: TAMORA
text: Nay, nay, sweet emperor, we must all be friends.

speaker: TAMORA
text: The tribune and his nephews kneel for grace.

speaker: TAMORA
text: I will not be denied. Sweetheart, look back.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Marcus, for thy sake, and thy brother’s here,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: And at my lovely Tamora’s entreats,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: I do remit these young men’s heinous faults.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Stand up.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Lavinia, though you left me like a churl,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: I found a friend, and sure as death I swore

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: I would not part a bachelor from the priest.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Come, if the Emperor’s court can feast two brides,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: You are my guest, Lavinia, and your friends.—

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: This day shall be a love-day, Tamora.

speaker: TITUS
text: Tomorrow, an it please your Majesty

speaker: TITUS
text: To hunt the panther and the hart with me,

speaker: TITUS
text: With horn and hound we’ll give your Grace bonjour.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Be it so, Titus, and gramercy too.
